verses.fhb_text: When Jesus had said these things, he lifted his eyes to heaven and said: "Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son, that the Son may glorify you,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Opening of the high priestly prayer — Jesus addresses the Father directly.

Renderings:
• "Father" — vocative preserved verbatim; Jesus's own address, not collapsed to "our Father" per FHB-1.2-21
• "your Son" / "the Son" — Father-Son distinction per FHB-1.2-1 speaker grammar
verses.footnote: John 17 is Jesus's longest recorded prayer to the Father — spoken the night before the cross, opening his heart to the One he calls Father, Holy Father, and righteous Father.
jesus_words: "Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son, that the Son may glorify you,

verses.fhb_text: since you have given him authority over all people, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus continues addressing the Father; "you" = the Father, "him" = the Son per speaker grammar.
jesus_words: since you have given him authority over all flesh, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.

verses.fhb_text: And this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true Father, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father named as "the only true God," distinguished from the Son whom he sent.

Renderings:
• "you, the only true God" — Jesus addresses the Father; the predicate is preserved as his own naming (FHB-1.2-21)
• "Jesus Christ" — Greek convention preserved per FHB-1.2-20

Hebrew/Greek: ton monon alēthinon theon → "the only true God" (Father is the referent, addressed as "you")
jesus_words: And this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true Father, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.

verses.fhb_text: I glorified you on the earth, having finished the work you gave me to do.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; "I" / "you" per speaker grammar.
jesus_words: I glorified you on the earth, having finished the work you gave me to do.

verses.fhb_text: And now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory I had with you before the world existed.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Son's pre-existent glory with the Father.

Renderings:
• "Father" — vocative preserved verbatim per FHB-1.2-21
• "the glory I had with you before the world existed" — eternal Father-Son fellowship
jesus_words: And now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory I had with you before the world existed.

verses.fhb_text: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me out of the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; "your name," "your word" preserved as direct address to the Father.
jesus_words: I have revealed your name to the people you gave me out of the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.

verses.fhb_text: Now they know that everything you have given me is from you,
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking to the Father; speaker grammar.
jesus_words: Now they know that everything you have given me is from you,

verses.fhb_text: for I have given them the words you gave me, and they received them and truly knew that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; the words given, the sending — all in the Father's direct address.
jesus_words: for I have given them the words you gave me, and they received them and truly knew that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.

verses.fhb_text: I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world, but for those you have given me, for they are yours.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus interceding before the Father; speaker grammar throughout.
jesus_words: I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world, but for those you have given me, for they are yours.

verses.fhb_text: All that is mine is yours, and what is yours is mine, and I am glorified in them.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Mutual possession between Father and Son.

Renderings:
• "All that is mine is yours, and what is yours is mine" — the Father-Son shared life preserved as Jesus speaks it
jesus_words: All that is mine is yours, and what is yours is mine, and I am glorified in them.

verses.fhb_text: I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name, which you have given me, that they may be one as we are one.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "Holy Father" — Jesus's distinctive vocative preserved verbatim.

Renderings:
• "Holy Father" — vocative kept exactly per FHB-1.2-21; not collapsed to "our Father"
• "that they may be one as we are one" — Father-Son unity extended to the disciples
jesus_words: I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name, which you have given me, that they may be one as we are one.

verses.fhb_text: While I was with them, I kept them in your name, which you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost except the son of destruction, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Judas as the son of destruction, fulfilling Scripture.

Renderings:
• "the son of destruction" — huios tēs apōleias preserved literally
• "so that the Scripture might be fulfilled" — Ps 41:9 / Ps 109:8 echo per FHB-1.2-23
verses.footnote: "The son of destruction" is a title for Judas Iscariot, the one who betrayed Jesus.

sources.net: When I was with them I kept them safe and watched over them in your name that you have given me. Not one of them was lost except the one destined for destruction, so that the scripture could be fulfilled.

gemini.literal_rendering: When I was with them, I was keeping them in your name which you have given me, and I guarded them, and not one of them was lost except the son of destruction, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

layer3.rationale: The FHB rendering accurately reflects the Greek: 'son of destruction' (huios tēs apōleias), 'I guarded them,' and the purpose clause 'so that the Scripture might be fulfilled'; 'might be' is standard English for the hina purpose clause and matches NET/ESV. No meaning error.
layer3.source_basis: NET Bible and reviewer model (literal rendering, faithful=true, high confidence) both confirm the rendering.

jesus_words: While I was with them, I kept them in your name, which you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost except the son of destruction, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

verses.fhb_text: But now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my complete joy in them.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking to the Father; "my joy made full" preserved.
jesus_words: But now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my joy made full in themselves.

verses.fhb_text: I have given them your word, and the world hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; "your word" preserved as direct address.
jesus_words: I have given them your word, and the world hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

verses.fhb_text: I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but to keep them from the evil one.
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• "the evil one" — tou ponērou rendered personally, not abstract "evil"
jesus_words: I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but to keep them from the evil one.

verses.fhb_text: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; parallel refrain to v.14.
jesus_words: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

verses.fhb_text: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's word as truth.

Renderings:
• "your word is truth" — preserved as Jesus's address to the Father
jesus_words: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.

verses.fhb_text: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; the sending mirrored from Father to Son to disciples.
jesus_words: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.

verses.fhb_text: And for their sake I sanctify myself, that they too may be sanctified in truth.
verses.decision_notes: Jesus speaking; self-consecration for the disciples.
jesus_words: And for their sake I sanctify myself, that they too may be sanctified in truth.

verses.fhb_text: I am not praying for these alone, but also for those who will believe in me through their word,
verses.decision_notes: Jesus broadens the prayer to all future believers.
jesus_words: I am not praying for these alone, but also for those who will believe in me through their word,

verses.fhb_text: that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I in you, that they also may be in us, so that the world may believe that you sent me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The unity of believers grounded in Father-Son oneness.

Renderings:
• "you, Father, are in me and I in you" — vocative preserved; the mutual indwelling per FHB-1.2-1
("father_son_unity" category already used — no footnote.)
jesus_words: that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I in you, that they also may be in us, so that the world may believe that you sent me.

verses.fhb_text: The glory you have given me I have given to them, that they may be one just as we are one—
verses.decision_notes:
Renderings:
• em dash closes the thought into v.23, a true continuation that a period would sever (FHB-9.5-1)
jesus_words: The glory you have given me I have given to them, that they may be one just as we are one—

verses.fhb_text: I in them and you in me, that they may be brought to complete unity, so that the world may know that you sent me and loved them even as you loved me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father loves believers with the same love he has for the Son.

Renderings:
• "loved them even as you loved me" — the Father's love for his children measured by his love for the Son
jesus_words: I in them and you in me, that they may be brought to complete unity, so that the world may know that you sent me and loved them even as you loved me.

verses.fhb_text: Father, I desire that those you have given me may be with me where I am, to see my glory that you have given me, because you loved me before the world was made.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's love for the Son before creation.

Renderings:
• "Father" — vocative preserved verbatim per FHB-1.2-21
• "before the foundation of the world" — pre-creation Father-Son love
jesus_words: Father, I desire that those you have given me may be with me where I am, to see my glory that you have given me, because you loved me before the foundation of the world.

verses.fhb_text: Righteous Father, though the world does not know you, I know you, and these know that you sent me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "Righteous Father" — Jesus's distinctive vocative preserved verbatim.

Renderings:
• "Righteous Father" — kept exactly per FHB-1.2-21; not collapsed to "our Father"
jesus_words: Righteous Father, though the world does not know you, I know you, and these know that you sent me.

verses.fhb_text: I have made your name known to them, and I will continue to make it known, so that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The prayer closes with the Father's love placed within the children.

Renderings:
• "the love with which you loved me may be in them" — the Father-Son love indwelling believers, the prayer's climax
jesus_words: I have made your name known to them, and I will continue to make it known, so that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.

chapter_notes: John 17, the high priestly prayer, is governed throughout by FHB-1.2-21: Jesus is the speaker addressing the Father, so speaker-grammar (FHB-1.2-1 v4.3) applies in every verse — "Father" / "my Father" / "Holy Father" (v.11) / "Righteous Father" (v.25) are preserved verbatim and never collapsed to the narrator default "our Father." One establishing footnote at v.1 per FHB-1.2-21 economy. The unity-theology footnote at v.21 was suppressed because "father_son_unity" is already in the used-category list. v.3 "the only true God" preserved as Jesus's own naming of the Father whom he addresses. No ungoverned decisions surfaced.
flags_summary: Layer 3 fidelity self-check: 1 verse(s) checked — 1 confirmed, 0 revised (applied), 0 suggested, 0 unresolved

headings.text: That They May All Be One
